<!-- GENERATED by `bun run gen` from scenarios/mysql/02-isolation/stable-snapshot.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

```
A> BEGIN;
Query OK
```

*The snapshot is taken by the FIRST query, not by BEGIN.*

```
A> SELECT id, balance FROM accounts ORDER BY id;
 id | balance 
----+---------
  1 |     100 
  2 |      50 
(2 rows)
```

*B changes an existing row AND inserts a new one — both committed instantly.*

```
B> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 999 WHERE id = 1;
Query OK, 1 row affected

B> INSERT INTO accounts VALUES (3, 'carol', 300);
Query OK, 1 row affected

A> SELECT id, balance FROM accounts ORDER BY id;
 id | balance 
----+---------
  1 |     100 
  2 |      50 
(2 rows)

A> COMMIT;
Query OK
```

*Only a NEW transaction gets a new snapshot.*

```
A> SELECT id, balance FROM accounts ORDER BY id;
 id | balance 
----+---------
  1 |     999 
  2 |      50 
  3 |     300 
(3 rows)
```

<small>Verified against MySQL 8.4.10 · [Run it yourself](/about/run-locally) · [Scenario source](https://github.com/svyatov/database-transactions/blob/main/scenarios/mysql/02-isolation/stable-snapshot.ts)</small>
